title: Clearlight Energy finances construction of 122 MW Canton MacNider Wind Farm Project in Quebec
content:
## Clearlight Energy finances construction of 122 MW Canton MacNider Wind Farm Project in Quebec
On January 7, 2026, Clearlight Energy completed the construction financing of the Canton MacNider Wind Farm Project in Quebec, which recently received authorization for construction from the Government of Québec. The project is a partnership between Clearlight Energy and the Alliance de l'énergie de l'Est and will include 20 wind turbines with a total installed capacity of 122.32 megawatts of renewable electricity.
Clearlight Energy is one of North America’s leading private renewable energy companies, owning and operating more than 3,700MW of utility-scale solar, wind, battery storage, and renewable natural gas facilities, with more than 1,200 MW in its development pipeline.
McCarthy Tétrault advised Clearlight Energy with a team led by Sean O’Neill that included Etienne Guertin, William Lee, Kean Silverthorn, Quentin Pérès (Financial Services), Gurvir Sangha, Sarah Xu (Corporate), Mary Pagonis, Xin Yue Zhang (RPP), Matthew Kraemer, Stefanie Morand, and Camille Belanger (Tax).
